title: Advertised Free '30-day history' retention is never enforced in code — phantom tier boundary
No dollar price for Pro — the only tier that would help her is undecidable from inside the product
Per-dimension trend moves shown without the noise/confidence caveat the headline trajectory carries
Recurring period summary restates the number on a stable repo instead of saying what changed
STRENGTH — trajectory is honest at low N: R² confidence + flat-floor surfaced where the move is shown
Pro subscription dollar amount is invisible in-app — the cost↔value math can't close
Recurring read is fleet-shaped; on a 1-repo monolith the 'movers / N repos leveled up' machinery degenerates
A guardband-sized wobble on an unchanged monolith shows as a delta with no noise caveat where the delta is shown
Credits persist (roll over) but the plan reads '100 / month' — a low-burn shop assumes 90+ idle credits are wasted
STRENGTH: a slow monolith reads as flat and labels its own noise — exactly the senior honesty Yusuf demands
STRENGTH: /usage makes per-cycle burn and the credit balance legible (the half of the math that IS shown)
Subscription price is invisible at her tier — she cannot close the board's ROI math
'365-day history' is a billed label with no wired retention floor — tier differentiation is illusory
Movers/period tiles show raw +N deltas with no confidence — re-scan noise reads identical to real movement
Movers name a dimension but not the team action that caused the gain — short of board-grade provenance
Stable Node fleet may flatline the trajectory into 'no level change projected' — repetition stops surfacing new
STRENGTH — board-credible trajectory: slope + ETA + R² confidence, all re-pullable as a same-shape briefing
48/500 credits is reachable on /usage, so overpay is visible — but with no $ she can only judge it half-way
No burn-vs-allotment ceiling signal — he learns about Team's 500 cap by hitting the 402, not before
Enterprise step-up is a value-blind 'Custom — contact us' at the exact moment of decision
The board briefing drops the trajectory's confidence signal — he could present model-noise as a trend
Advertised tier retention ('365-day history') isn't what governs the trajectory window
STRENGTH — definition-stable quarterly number + delta-with-provenance make the board read defensible
STRENGTH — /usage already meters burn + runway, so the ceiling signal is one derivation away
Gradle Kotlin-DSL build manifests (build.gradle.kts / settings.gradle.kts / libs.versions.toml) are not picked or detected — a .kts-only JVM repo is under-read identically every cycle
No fleet-wide adoption-curve / movement view: overview movers are a top-5 teaser and the 200-repo table is unsorted-by-movement and unpaginated
Per-repo row score moves carry no real-vs-noise label; the flat-floor/R² defense lives only on the fleet trajectory
Peer-cohort benchmark keys on GitHub primaryLanguage, which is unstable for mixed Kotlin/Java Gradle repos

STRENGTH: the recurring read has a real noise defense — flat-floor + R² 'trend confidence · noisy' surfaced exactly where the move is shown, and the trajectory requires ≥2 days so repetition genuinely earns the feature
STRENGTH: PeriodSummary gives the one re-pullable sentence a VP wants — net maturity delta, promoted/demoted counts, onboarded-this-period — cohort-matched so it isn't diluted by new repos
Weekly digest fires on a fixed cron with no movement gate — emails 'no change this week' and trains the inbox filter
Default regression threshold (5 pts) sits inside the ±25 scoring guardband — alerts can fire on model wobble, not real movement
No per-org digest cadence — a director on a quarterly renewal rhythm gets a weekly drip he can't retune
STRENGTH: the digest is a self-contained, forward-it-up artifact, not a bare dashboard link
STRENGTH: unchanged-commit dedup means a re-scan of a literally-unchanged repo fires zero alerts
No bulk export of the org-level scores / movers / trajectory — the recurring-value payload itself isn't portable
Per-repo movers show raw deltas with no 'is this real vs guardband noise' signal — only the headline trajectory is defended
No subscription $ for the tier she'd actually buy — a build-vs-buy ledger needs a number, and Pro/Team show only 'Prepaid'
The headline scoring (weighted mean + ±25 guardband + 60/40 blend + OLS slope) is reproducible in-house — the moat rests almost entirely on the corpus
STRENGTH: the cross-org corpus percentile + an honest trajectory confidence are the two things she can't trivially build
White-label is Enterprise-only — Bruno (Team) can never brand the resold report
Exported briefing still says 'Scored by Ascent' (footer + filename) even when white-labelled
No per-client (per-segment) briefing — the resold report can only be the whole-org blend
No visible subscription price or per-client cost — can't compute a markup
Trend confidence (R²) isn't surfaced on the exec briefing where the move is narrated
STRENGTH — 'vs previous period' + movers + forecast give a genuinely new monthly story
STRENGTH — per-client separation + account-free share are reachable on Team
No subscription price or idle-floor in-app — a burst buyer can't tell if she'd pay between deals
Confident ETA fires off a noisy short-window fit — undefensible in a deal memo
Small dimension deltas reported as movement with no 'within noise' guard
Marketed tier retention (30/180/365d) is decorative — not the enforcement mechanism
STRENGTH — exportable, tier-agnostic deal artifact (PDF + markdown + CSV) enables a clean export-and-cancel
STRENGTH — trajectory refuses to render without ≥2 distinct days, and flags low-R² fits as 'noisy'
No fleet-of-fleets / cross-company view — assembling 15 portcos onto one comparable slide is still manual
Archetype lens varies the weights across companies, but cross-company comparability is never caveated
Trend-confidence (R²/noisy) lives on the Trajectory card only — movers, period deltas, and the briefing present movement numbers with no confidence attached
Enterprise price is 'Custom — contact us' — value↔cost can't be computed self-serve
STRENGTH — quarterly cadence is honestly caveated: R² 'noisy' flag + FLAT_PER_WEEK floor stop a confident line through 4 dots
STRENGTH — per-company executive briefing is genuinely IC-deck-grade and shareable
`retentionDays` is dead metadata — the 365-day/custom retention she's upgrading for is enforced by NO code path
Audit log + history export are append-by-convention, not tamper-evident — examiner can't attest the recurring record wasn't altered
No noise defense co-located with the D9 score — a re-scan wobble within ±25 reads as a real supply-chain regression in the evidence pack
Subscription $ for Team→Enterprise is invisible, and the one axis she's deciding on (retention) is 'Custom — contact us'
STRENGTH: D9 definition + cohort-matched period deltas are genuinely examiner-grade
STRENGTH — the inference hop is disclosed in-product, per-engine, and separated from persistence
STRENGTH — every scan records its engine, so a degraded/mixed-engine cycle is visible, not laundered into the trend
STRENGTH (with caveat) — trajectory/movers/blend are engine-agnostic, so the recurring read survives Bedrock or the mock floor
The privacy-safe engine is a deployment-level Phase-2 choice, not a per-org toggle — a Pro-tier Owen can't actually select Bedrock on the SaaS
No BYO-model / self-host / on-prem inference story beyond Bedrock or mock — the most-sensitive repos have only the hollow floor
Subscription $ is invisible for Pro/Team — Owen can count credits but can't see what the privacy-capable tier costs to decide
If forced to mock, the recurring score is repeatable and labeled — but loses the LLM nuance/discrepancy-catch that makes it senior-grade
No scan engine runs air-gapped with model quality — claude-cli and bedrock phone home; only the deterministic mock is offline
GitHub host is hardcoded to api.github.com — on-prem GitHub Enterprise Server behind the firewall is unreachable
When the real engine fails at the boundary, the scan degrades to the deterministic floor — but the recurring artifact doesn't record that the model didn't run
The exportable 'evidence' is a scan-event CSV, not a maturity attestation with cited per-dimension evidence
Enterprise price is 'Custom — contact us'; recurring artifact value can't be mapped to the contract line from inside the app
STRENGTH — Enterprise custom retention + R²-gated trajectory makes the quarter-over-quarter read structurally sound IF it can run
STRENGTH — audit CSV export is org-scoped, keyset-paginated, and CSV-injection-hardened (real compliance hygiene)
No OSS-shaped paid hook — a public-repo-only steward gets unlimited recurring value with zero conversion path (monetization gap)
"Unlimited public-repo scans" is actually a soft weekly cap (3 anon / 20 signed-in) — pricing copy overstates Free
30-day retention is unenforced — Free silently gets MORE history than advertised (generosity overshoot)
Free recurring read has the trajectory line but no narrated 'what moved since last cycle' — period-movers are Pro+
STRENGTH — badge auto-refreshes on push and labels the mock floor '· demo'; the free recurring signal is honest and Scorecard-shaped
Movers panel shows raw deltas with no noise-vs-signal defense — the R²/confidence guard never reaches where the move is shown
Stable-fleet plateau — trajectory flatlines to 'no level change projected' and movers go empty; cycle N re-dates cycle N-1
No subscription price for paid tiers — the renewal math can't be modeled at all
Free tier is structurally un-sticky — no between-login touch and a 30-day trajectory that can't compound
STRENGTH — Trajectory GPS is history-required and surfaces its own R² with a 'noisy' flag (the sticky asset done right)
STRENGTH (the moats Camille can't replicate) — cross-org/cohort percentile + the .ai standard adoption loop
No ML/notebook archetype — the 9-dim rubric is applied unchanged to research repos
Notebooks are invisible end-to-end — ingestion, detectors, and the LLM prompt all skip .ipynb
Roadmap recommends web-dev hygiene (add tests / CI / conventional commits) to research repos
Month-over-month trend is precise but pointed at a mismeasured number — repetition compounds the mis-fit
No 'this rubric is tuned for app repos' caveat — the tool never names its ML blind spot
STRENGTH — movers + trajectory + R²/flat-floor are genuinely good recurring-read machinery
STRENGTH — at Team, credits & retention are not the constraint (40/500 used, 365-day window)
D3 CI/CD & Delivery is blind to mobile delivery — reads release trains as a generic JVM build
Real-vs-noise is surfaced on the org trajectory but NOT on a single dimension move
STRENGTH — bi-weekly cadence yields a real trajectory with honest flat-floor + surfaced R²
STRENGTH — movers report cohort-matched 'since last period', not a restated current number
No subscription $ for Team — she can see credits/retention but not what she's renewing for
STRENGTH: flat trajectory is framed as a verdict ('Holding around N — no level change projected'), not a blank
STRENGTH: an unchanged-commit autoscan dedups, refunds the credit, and suppresses the regression alert — cost tracks new info, not the calendar
The 'movers' banner states a score delta with no noise/confidence guard — a guardband wobble reads as a real climb
No embedded/safety-critical archetype; L5-Autonomous framed as the goal for code that legitimately can't ship autonomously
Renewing Pro against an invisible price — only 'prepaid credits', no subscription $ in-app
Trajectory needs 2+ distinct calendar days; a repo that changes a few times a quarter starves the fit
No human-engagement signal — /usage proves the cron ran, never that a person opened the dashboard
No consolidated value-realization line — 'you actioned N recs, moved +X points this quarter' must be hand-assembled
Actioned-rec count under-reports on the live-LLM path (title-drift re-scan match) — value-realization reads low
Price for Team is off-app — can't compute $/actioned-move for the CFO sheet without leaving for Polar
Rising scan-volume chart can read as 'healthy usage' while logins are flat — vanity-metric trap
STRENGTH — recommendation action IS tracked, attributed, and tenant-gated (real value-realization primitive)
Backlog 'Done' is cumulative, not period-scoped — overstates this-cycle activity at renewal
/usage shows credit burn but never against the 500 included allotment — no utilization, so the tier can't be right-sized in-product
'500 / month' copy over a persisted, rolling-over credit pool — the meter contradicts the marketing, in the vendor's favor but silently
Pro/Team show no subscription price, so $/scan has a blank denominator — 'worth the price' is literally uncomputable for the tier I'm on
No over/under-provisioning ('wrong tier') nudge despite the app holding both burn and allotment
STRENGTH: credit reconciliation (debited / refunded / granted / net vs ledger) is exactly the audit row a FinOps owner trusts
Per-credit value depends on each cycle surfacing something new — the noise/guardband defense (R²/flat-floor) isn't co-located with the move

CONSOLIDATED — the subscription price is invisible at the moment of the renew/upgrade decision: /pricing shows only 'Prepaid — credits, 1 per private scan' for Pro/Team and 'Custom — contact us' for Enterprise; the $ lives in Polar, never in the app
expected: A character judging 'is the repetition worth what I pay' can see a dollar figure (subscription $ or per-credit $) for their tier, in-app, so the cost↔value math closes without a sales detour.
got: plans.ts carries no dollar amounts by design; /pricing renders prepaid-credits framing with no $ for Pro/Team and 'Custom — contact us' for Enterprise. 16 of 20 characters independently hit this. Credit BURN is legible on /usage; the subscription PRICE (the denominator of $/scan and $/actioned-move) is structurally absent. Cross-cohort: blocks the cost↔value decision for every paid-considering buyer; only the Enterprise-by-design buyers (Robert/Theo/Diane) shrug it off because procurement owns the number.
l2_priority: From /pricing + /usage under the bypass, confirm no subscription $ for Pro/Team and 'Custom' for Enterprise — the cost↔value math is undecidable in-app for any paid tier.

title: CONSOLIDATED — `retentionDays` (30/180/365/null) is dead metadata: declared in plans.ts, advertised on /pricing per tier, but read by NO query or purge — the history/trend/movers queries apply only a row-count clamp (≤200), and the real purge reads only retentionMaxScans/retentionAuditDays
expected: The retention window each tier sells (Free 30 / Pro 180 / Team 365 / Ent custom) actually bounds how far back the trajectory/history can look (or what is purged) — a real, enforced, attestable tier boundary.
got: retentionDays is referenced only in plans.ts + /pricing display. The trend/forecast query (org-rollup.ts) and per-repo history (scans-read.ts, ≤200 rows) apply no plan-derived date floor; the purge (retention.ts / cron/purge) reads only Organization.retentionMaxScans / retentionAuditDays (opt-in, default 0 = keep everything). So the trajectory looks back as far as data exists on ANY tier. Independently verified by 6 characters (Priyanka phantom-cap, Lena/Gabriel illusory-differentiation, Helena decorative, Mariam phantom-upgrade-axis, Kenji inverse-generosity-overshoot). Team & Enterprise are indistinguishable on the one retention axis Mariam is deciding her upgrade on; Free silently over-delivers.
l2_priority: On a populated org, confirm a scan older than the tier's advertised window still enters the trajectory fit and is not purged under Team AND Free — proving retentionDays gates nothing in either direction.

title: CONSOLIDATED — the move-is-real defense (R²/'trend confidence · noisy' + FLAT_PER_WEEK floor) lives ONLY on the Trajectory card; the movers/PeriodSummary tiles, the executive briefing, per-dimension rows, per-repo leaderboard rows, and the regression alert all show raw +N deltas with no noise annotation
expected: Everywhere a per-cycle +N delta is shown, it carries a signal-vs-noise cue — because the LLM is guardbanded ±25 to the deterministic signal and blended 60/40 (so an unchanged repo re-scanned under claude-cli can wobble up to ~±15/dim) — so a guardband wobble isn't read (or pasted into a board slide / examiner pack / client report) as a real move.
got: fitQuality (R²) + the '· noisy' tag + the flat-floor render only inside <Trajectory>. getOrgMovers/PeriodSummary surface raw dOverall; the executive briefing renders only forecastHeadline text; per-dimension rows, RepoLeaderboard rows, and the DEFAULT regression threshold (5 pts, inside the ±25 band) carry no confidence. 15 of 20 characters independently flagged this as the recurring trust wound — the single most-raised defect after the invisible price. It is the same root mis-routing across surfaces (Gabriel: board briefing; Robert: regression alert ≤ guardband; Sasha/Camille/Lena: movers; Mariam: D9; Sofia: D3; Anika: per-repo rows; Helena: noisy ETA).
l2_priority: Re-scan an UNCHANGED repo twice under claude-cli; confirm dOverall (and a per-dimension delta) moves within the ±25 guardband, surfaces as a 'mover'/regression with no noise tag anywhere outside the Trajectory card — the cross-cohort churn vector.

title: CONSOLIDATED — credits are a persisted rollover pool (org.scanCredits, a single Int, never reset, no monthly-reset cron) but /pricing advertises 'N private scans / month' — the meter is more generous than the marketing, silently, and the burst buyer can't confirm there's no idle floor
expected: The app states plainly whether credits roll over (a prepaid pool) or reset monthly (use-it-or-lose-it) — it flips the budgeting math, defangs the overpay fear for low-burn shops, and lets a burst buyer (Helena) confirm 'no idle subscription floor' from inside the product.
got: Organization.scanCredits is one persisted integer that grantCredits/consumeScanCredit only increment/decrement — no period/reset/billingCycle field and no reset cron exist; credits debit 1 per private scan with no coded monthly floor. Yet plans.ts/'pricing' say 'N / month', implying expiry. Yusuf fears torched credits, Victor had to read the schema to learn rollover favors him, Helena can't prove the absence of a recurring floor from the surface so she prices it as a year-round subscription against 6 weeks of use and churns.
l2_priority: Grant credits, consume some, advance a month (or re-seed); confirm the balance does NOT reset — credits are a rolling pool — while '/month' copy stands and no UI string says 'roll over'.

title: CONSOLIDATED — the archetype lens is solo/team/org only (classified by stars + CODEOWNERS + workflows; language/stack never enters), and the detectors/ingestion are web/JVM-Groovy-CI-centric — so non-web stacks are read by a rubric that doesn't fit them, identically every cycle (a stable-but-WRONG baseline the trend then faithfully tracks)
expected: A repo is judged against a stack-appropriate lens that reads its actual signals (Gradle .kts builds, .ipynb notebooks, mobile release pipelines, embedded/safety-critical posture), or at minimum surfaces a 'partial fit for <stack>' caveat — so repetition compounds a maturity, not a mismeasurement.
got: RepoArchetype = solo|team|org, classified by stars/CODEOWNERS/workflow count with language never entering. The pickers/detectors know build.gradle (Groovy) not build.gradle.kts/libs.versions.toml (Anika); skip .ipynb in ingestion + D2 SOURCE_PATH + the prompt (Arjun); D3 has zero fastlane/Xcode Cloud/TestFlight/signing vocabulary (Sofia); no embedded/safety-critical lens and L5-Autonomous framed as the apex for code that legally can't ship autonomously (Klaus). Because each miss is deterministic, the repeated score is stable AND wrong — worse than noise, since it can't be caught by eye — and no stack-fit caveat ever names the blind spot. For Arjun this is the run's only L1-FAIL (CHURN).
l2_priority: Scan a .kts-only JVM repo, a .ipynb-dominant ML repo, a fastlane/TestFlight mobile repo, and an embedded C/C++ repo under claude-cli; confirm each is under-read the SAME way on re-scan (stably-wrong) and no stack-fit caveat renders.

title: CONSOLIDATED — monetization & value-realization leaks: the product gives away or fails to capture recurring value at the exact decision points — no OSS-shaped paid hook (Kenji never converts), white-label/per-client resale blocked on Team (Bruno can't resell), no burn-vs-allotment utilization or tier-fit nudge (Victor/Gabriel can't right-size and learn the ceiling by hitting the 402), and no human-engagement or org-level value-realization line (Tania can't prove anyone used it at renewal)
expected: At each recurring decision moment the product surfaces its own value and a path to capture/justify it: a conversion hook for high-value free users, a resale-ready per-client branded deliverable, a utilization-vs-allotment right-size read, and a 'who used it / what it earned this period' value-realization line.
got: All-public stewards get unlimited free recurring value with zero conversion path (the only paid surface gates on PRIVATE scans + credits). Team's segments fit per-client work but white-label is Enterprise-only and even then leaks 'Scored by Ascent'. /usage shows burn but never against includedCredits (no 'X of 500', no over/under-provision nudge); the tier ceiling is learned via the 402. There is no lastLoginAt/dashboard-open telemetry anywhere and no org-level 'recs actioned this period + points moved' line — so the renewal-justifying engagement read is unanswerable in-app. The recurring machinery is strong; the business layer that turns it into defended revenue leaks at every decision point.
l2_priority: Confirm: an all-public org reaches /trends+badge+report indefinitely at $0 with no upgrade prompt; Team's branding form is absent/403; /usage renders no 'X of 500' utilization; no human last-active/active-users stat exists on any org surface.
